refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly focus on the latency metric and its impact on safe teleoperation, clarifying the performance requirement for verification and design.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation scenarios; verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low processing delay.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocols and data encoding to reduce latency.
DP2: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees for teleoperation traffic.
DP3: Implement edge processing modules to minimize processing delays in telemetry and tasking.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Optimize communication protocols and data encoding to reduce latency.

potential_sources: Coupling between telemetry data processing and network link performance
Dependency on safety services for alerting on latency breaches
coupling_check.notes: DP1 focuses on communication protocol optimization, which is logically separable from network infrastructure and safety alerting, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link can meet baseline performance requirements.
Risk that protocol optimization alone may not achieve latency target under all conditions.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent refinement levels to address network and processing delays explicitly.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication protocols, data encoding/decoding, and latency optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange with Vehicle module for real-time status and control.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands from NGHE Solution or Teleoperation control center.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface to physical network infrastructure ensuring data transmission.

module_containment_scores.notes: High-level system module; contains coordination but not detailed teleoperation logic.
Vehicle handles telemetry endpoints but not protocol optimization.
Primary module for teleoperation communication logic and latency optimization.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not implement teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency optimization logic is best contained within the Teleoperation module to centralize communication protocol management and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential latency impact from network link outside Teleoperation module control.
Risk of interface complexity if Teleoperation module depends heavily on Vehicle telemetry processing.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review network link performance and consider tighter integration or interface standardization with Vehicle and Safety Services modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (protocol optimization) directly addresses the refined FR of latency reduction with clear verification metrics.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if latency-sensitive processing is split across modules, but current containment in Teleoperation is strong.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows high containment score and low diffusion, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and network latency measurements is feasible with current interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency optimization responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Further refine sub-FRs to explicitly address network link and processing delays.
Evaluate integration points with Safety Services for latency breach alerts.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement is needed to explicitly address network link and processing delays as separate FRs and DPs to reduce residual risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low processing delay.

revise.why: To explicitly separate latency contributors and enable targeted DP and IM assignments for network and processing delays.

assumptions: Network link baseline performance meets minimum requirements.
Telemetry and tasking interfaces support low-latency data exchange.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative network conditions.
Interface performance benchmarks between Teleoperation and Vehicle.
